paper_id: Fumagalli et al. - 2022 - Conceptual model visual simulation and the inductive learning of missing domain constraints

models.model_label: OntoUML Criminal Investigation running example (Mary's model)

evidence_quotes.quote: More precisely, this paper develops an approach that automatically suggests repairing solutions for an under-constrained (Onto)UML model given a set of model configurations that have been identified by the modeler as intended or unintended.
She needs to find out which constructs in her model are allowing for unintended instances and come up with a solution to prevent them, while still allowing all intended instances she already checked.
As we show here, the approach is able to support the automated learning of constraints that are needed to rectify a number of relevant anti-patterns in this catalog.

goals.justification: The model is used to identify missing constraints, shortcomings, and anti-patterns, and to derive repairing solutions/decisions about what should change in the model. This matches the signal patterns 'identify shortcomings', 'what should change', and 'evaluate' for Problem-solving. The model itself is not the implementation instrument.

evidence_quotes.quote: The running example we adopted is designed to collect in one single model issues that, as described by the empirical analysis in [12], have been found in dozens of models (also used for real-world applications). This also allows us to validate how our approach can handle constraints that are needed in practice.

To validate that our approach can learn relevant constraints that are likely to be needed in practice, we use, as a ''litmus test'', the catalog of empirically-elicited ontological anti-patterns developed for OntoUML [12,49-51].
As the basis of our test we used the input model specified in Fig. 1 and we grouped all the errors that are admitted by that model into a set of ontological anti-patterns,

goals.justification: The Criminal Investigation model was explicitly designed as a test case to learn/validate how modeling constraints and anti-patterns can be acquired through the proposed approach. This is learning about the MODELING activity (OntoUML constraints, anti-patterns, modeling methods), matching the Learning signal patterns 'trial model', 'learn OntoUML', and 'modeling method'.

evidence_quotes.quote: She knows the model is syntactically correct and logically consistent, but before sharing it, Mary wants to make sure it also accurately represents the domain she modeled.
To play their role as instruments for domain modeling, conceptual models must contain the exact set of constraints that represent the worldview of the relevant domain stakeholders.

uncertain_goals.reason: While the scenario frames the model as representing a domain worldview, the focus is on validating/repairing constraints (problem-solving) rather than on clarifying or negotiating shared meaning about the domain itself; the domain (criminal investigation) is incidental rather than the object of understanding.

evidence_quotes.quote: The configurations are generated by converting the OntoUML model at hand into an Alloy specification (activity 1.a in Fig. 3), which is then fed into the Alloy Analyzer to generate possible instances or to look for counter-examples that fail the assumptions we have about our model (activity 1.b in Fig. 3).

uncertain_goals.reason: The model is transformed into Alloy specifications as part of the validation pipeline, but this transformation is in service of validation/learning rather than building a system or artifact in the world; the Repair phase that would change the model is explicitly deferred to future work.
